steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: From the graph, the line crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,6)$ and the $x$-axis at $(2,0)$. These two intercepts are enough to avoid a long slope-intercept setup: the correct answer must pass through both points with a positive value of $R$.

steps.title: Check choice C directly
steps.content: Use the $y$-intercept in choice C: $18x+Ry=36$. Substituting $(0,6)$ gives $6R=36$, so $R=6$, which is positive. With that value, choice C becomes $$18x+6y=36$$ This line also passes through $(2,0)$ because $18(2)+6(0)=36$.

steps.title: Rule out the other forms
steps.content: Choices A and B put the fixed coefficient $18$ on the $y$-term, so at $(0,6)$ they force $18(6)=36$, which is false. Choice D has the wrong sign: at the x-intercept it gives $18(2)-R(0)=-36$, or $36=-36$. Only choice C matches both intercepts.
